attractions.name: Sigiriya Rock Fortress
attractions.description: Sigiriya or Sinhagiri is an ancient rock fortress located in the northern Matale District near the town of Dambulla in the Central Province, Sri Lanka. It is a site of historical and archaeological significance that is dominated by a massive column of granite approximately 180 metres high. The name refers to a site of historical and archaeological significance that is dominated by a massive column of rock nearly 200 metres high. According to the ancient Sri Lankan chronicle the Culavamsa, this site was selected by King Kashyapa for his new capital.
attractions.shortDescription: UNESCO World Heritage ancient rock fortress rising 200m above the jungle

attractions.name: Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic
attractions.description: Sri Dalada Maligawa or the Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic is a Buddhist temple in Kandy, Sri Lanka. It is located in the royal palace complex of the former Kingdom of Kandy, which houses the relic of the tooth of the Buddha. Since ancient times, the relic has played an important role in local politics because it is believed that whoever holds the relic holds the governance of the country.
attractions.shortDescription: Sacred Buddhist temple housing the relic of Buddha's tooth

attractions.name: Galle Fort
attractions.description: Galle Fort, in the Bay of Galle on the southwest coast of Sri Lanka, was first fortified by the Portuguese in 1588. The present-day fort was extensively rebuilt by the Dutch East India Company beginning in 1649. It is a historical, archaeological and architectural heritage monument, which even after more than 432 years maintains a pristine appearance. The fort has a colourful and fascinating history, and today is a thriving community of cafes, boutique shops, and heritage hotels.
attractions.shortDescription: 16th-century colonial fortress blending Dutch and Portuguese heritage

attractions.name: Yala National Park
attractions.description: Yala National Park is the most visited and second largest national park in Sri Lanka. The park consists of five blocks, two of which are now open to the public. It is situated in the southeast region of the country, and lies in Southern and Uva Provinces. The park covers 979 square kilometres and is home to 215 bird species and 44 mammal species. It has the highest leopard density in the world.
attractions.shortDescription: Sri Lanka's premier wildlife park with the highest leopard density on earth

attractions.name: Ella Rock & Nine Arches Bridge
attractions.description: Ella is a small town in the Badulla District of Uva Province, Sri Lanka. It is surrounded by hills covered with cloud forests and tea plantations. The town has a cooler climate than surrounding lowlands, due to its elevation. The area has a rich bio-diversity. The Nine Arches Bridge is one of the finest examples of colonial-era railway construction in the country, built in 1921 entirely of stone, brick, and cement without any steel.
attractions.shortDescription: Misty mountain town with iconic railway bridge and dramatic hiking trails

attractions.name: Mirissa Beach
attractions.description: Mirissa is a small town on the south coast of Sri Lanka, located in the Matara District of the Southern Province. It is the main whale and dolphin watching spot in Sri Lanka. The beach is one of the finest in the southern coast, known for its golden sand, turquoise waters, and laid-back atmosphere. Coconut palms line the shore, and the iconic Parrot Rock provides a stunning sunset viewpoint.
attractions.shortDescription: Golden-sand paradise beach known for whale watching and epic sunsets

attractions.name: Shangri-La Colombo
attractions.description: Shangri-La Hotel, Colombo is an iconic luxury hotel located along the Galle Face waterfront in the heart of Colombo. This modern five-star establishment offers 500 elegantly appointed rooms and suites with stunning ocean and city views. Guests can enjoy a range of world-class dining options, a serene spa, an outdoor pool overlooking the Indian Ocean, and state-of-the-art fitness facilities.
attractions.shortDescription: Five-star oceanfront luxury hotel on the Galle Face waterfront

attractions.name: Heritance Kandalama
attractions.description: Heritance Kandalama is an award-winning eco-luxury hotel designed by the legendary architect Geoffrey Bawa. Built into a cliff face overlooking Kandalama Reservoir and the distant Sigiriya Rock, the hotel seamlessly blends modernist architecture with the surrounding jungle. Over the years, the building has been reclaimed by nature, with vines and foliage creeping over its concrete facade.
attractions.shortDescription: Geoffrey Bawa's legendary eco-luxury hotel carved into jungle cliffs

attractions.name: Adam's Peak (Sri Pada)
attractions.description: Adam's Peak is a 2,243-metre tall conical mountain located in central Sri Lanka. It is well known for the Sri Pada, a 1.8 m rock formation near the summit, which in Buddhist tradition is held to be the footprint of the Buddha. The pilgrimage season runs from December to May. The pre-dawn hike is one of Sri Lanka's most exhilarating experiences, rewarding climbers with a spectacular sunrise above the clouds.
attractions.shortDescription: Sacred 2,243m peak with legendary footprint and breathtaking sunrise

attractions.name: Anuradhapura Ancient City
attractions.description: Anuradhapura is a major city in Sri Lanka and the ancient capital that lasted for over a thousand years. It is one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in the world and one of the eight World Heritage Sites of Sri Lanka. The city contains ruins of an ancient civilization, including massive dagobas (stupas), ancient pools, and the sacred Jaya Sri Maha Bodhi tree, believed to be the oldest historically authenticated tree in the world.
attractions.shortDescription: Ancient capital with 2,500-year-old ruins and the world's oldest sacred tree

attractions.name: Cinnamon Grand Colombo
attractions.description: Cinnamon Grand Colombo is one of the largest five-star hotels in Colombo, offering 501 rooms and suites along with an impressive array of 15 restaurants and bars. Located on Galle Road in the heart of the city, it is the perfect base for business and leisure travellers. The hotel features a stunning outdoor pool, a world-class spa, and is just minutes from major attractions.
attractions.shortDescription: Iconic Colombo five-star hotel with 15 restaurants and vibrant nightlife

attractions.name: Horton Plains & World's End
attractions.description: Horton Plains National Park is a protected area in the central highlands of Sri Lanka. It is covered by montane grassland and cloud forest, and is home to many species endemic to Sri Lanka. The park's most famous feature is World's End, a sheer precipice with a drop of about 880 metres. Baker's Falls is another popular attraction within the park. The park sits at an altitude of over 2,100 metres.
attractions.shortDescription: Misty highlands with a dramatic 880m cliff drop at World's End

attractions.name: 98 Acres Resort & Spa
attractions.description: 98 Acres Resort & Spa is a boutique eco-luxury resort perched on the edge of a tea estate in Ella, offering breathtaking panoramic views of Ella Gap and the surrounding mountains. The resort features 40 chalets built from reclaimed railway sleepers and natural stone, blending rustic charm with modern luxury. Each chalet has a private balcony with uninterrupted views of the misty mountains and lush tea plantations.
attractions.shortDescription: Boutique tea-estate resort with panoramic views of misty Ella Gap

attractions.name: Polonnaruwa Ancient City
attractions.description: Polonnaruwa is the second most ancient of Sri Lanka's kingdoms. The majestic ruins of the ancient city reflect the discipline, grandeur and artistry of the first rulers of this land. The Gal Vihara, with its stunning rock-carved Buddha statues, is considered a masterpiece of Sinhalese art. The well-preserved ruins include the Royal Palace, the Audience Hall, and the beautiful Lotus Bath.
attractions.shortDescription: Medieval royal capital with masterful rock-carved Buddha statues

attractions.name: Sinharaja Forest Reserve
attractions.description: Sinharaja Forest Reserve is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a national wilderness area in Sri Lanka. The hilly virgin rainforest is of great ecological importance. It is the country's last viable area of primary tropical rainforest, home to over 50% of Sri Lanka's endemic species. Walking through the forest with a guide reveals incredible biodiversity including rare birds, reptiles, and amphibians found nowhere else on Earth.
attractions.shortDescription: Last virgin tropical rainforest in Sri Lanka, a UNESCO biodiversity hotspot
